tests.test_name: Submit button is disabled when the queue is empty
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card

test_hooks.name: Given the analysis API is mocked; And the multi-file API is mocked; And I am on the Analyze page
test_hooks.explanation: Mocks both the analysis and multi-file APIs and navigates to the Analyze page before each scenario.

tests.journey: A logged-in user enables multi-file mode and sees the submit button disabled when no files have been added to the queue.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Submit button is disabled when the queue is empty
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: Analysis and multi-file APIs are mocked; user is on the Analyze page

tests.test_name: Submitting a valid multi-file batch shows the batch status card
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card

test_hooks.name: Given the analysis API is mocked; And the multi-file API is mocked; And I am on the Analyze page
test_hooks.explanation: Mocks both the analysis and multi-file APIs and navigates to the Analyze page before each scenario.

tests.journey: A logged-in user enables multi-file mode, configures format and role, adds a file, submits the batch, and verifies the batch status card appears while the results panel remains hidden.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Submitting a valid multi-file batch shows the batch status card
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: Analysis and multi-file APIs are mocked; user is on the Analyze page
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User enables multi-file mode, selects format 'bdd' and role 'Product Owner', adds a fixture file, and clicks submit
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: The batch status card is visible and the results panel is not visible

tests.test_name: Batch status card shows the job reference and polling message
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card

test_hooks.name: Given the analysis API is mocked; And the multi-file API is mocked; And I am on the Analyze page
test_hooks.explanation: Mocks both the analysis and multi-file APIs and navigates to the Analyze page before each scenario.

tests.journey: A logged-in user submits a valid multi-file batch and verifies that the resulting batch status card displays both a job ID reference and a polling status message.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Batch status card shows the job reference and polling message
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: Analysis and multi-file APIs are mocked; user is on the Analyze page
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User enables multi-file mode, selects format 'bdd' and role 'Product Owner', adds a fixture file, and clicks submit
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: The batch status card is visible, shows a job_id reference, and shows the polling message

tests.test_name: Batch status card remains visible after switching back to single-file mode
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card

test_hooks.name: Given the analysis API is mocked; And the multi-file API is mocked; And I am on the Analyze page
test_hooks.explanation: Mocks both the analysis and multi-file APIs and navigates to the Analyze page before each scenario.

tests.journey: A logged-in user submits a multi-file batch, then switches back to single-file mode, and verifies the batch status card persists and is not dismissed by the mode toggle.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Batch status card remains visible after switching back to single-file mode
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: Analysis and multi-file APIs are mocked; user is on the Analyze page; a batch has been submitted and the status card is visible
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: The batch status card is still visible

tests.test_name: Batch status card summary row shows format, role and file count
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card

test_hooks.name: Given the analysis API is mocked; And the multi-file API is mocked; And I am on the Analyze page
test_hooks.explanation: Mocks both the analysis and multi-file APIs and navigates to the Analyze page before each scenario.

tests.journey: A logged-in user submits a single-file multi-file batch and verifies that the batch status card summary row accurately reflects the selected format, role, and a file count of one.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Batch status card summary row shows format, role and file count
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: Analysis and multi-file APIs are mocked; user is on the Analyze page
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User enables multi-file mode, selects format 'bdd' and role 'Product Owner', adds one fixture file, and clicks submit
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: The batch status card shows the format label, the role label 'Product Owner', and a file count of '1'
